youtube_items.title: [YT] Global Health & Tropical Medicine Explained | Diseases, Poverty & Health Systems
youtube_items.summary:
Welcome to the learning studio. You're watching 
the health sciences series where we break down   the most important fields in medicine in a 
way that's easy to understand and exciting   to learn. In today's episode, we're traveling 
across borders, continents, and climate zones.   Because today, we're diving into global health 
and tropical medicine, the field where doctors,   scientists, and health workers fight diseases 
that affect millions in some of the world's   most vulnerable regions. What is global health and 
tropical medicine? Imagine if you were a doctor,   not just in one hospital, but in a world full 
of different countries, cultures, climates,   and diseases. Global health is about solving 
health problems that go beyond borders. From   pandemics like CO 19 to childhood mal

youtube_items.title: [YT] Is inequality inevitable?
youtube_items.summary:
In South Africa, one of the most
unequal countries in the world, the richest one-tenth of 1%, owns 
almost 30% of all the country’s wealth, more than double what the bottom 90% owns. Income and wealth inequality are not new. In fact, economists and historians 
who’ve charted economic inequality throughout history haven’t found 
a single society without it. Which raises a bleak question: is inequality inevitable? One way to estimate inequality is 
with a number called the Gini index, which is calculated by comparing 
the income or wealth distribution of a perfectly equal society to the 
actual income or wealth distribution. The area of this shape multiplied
by 2 is the Gini index. A Gini of 1 indicates
perfect inequality— one person has everything 
and everyone else has nothing. You’d never

youtube_items.title: [YT] Why More Americans Are Being Pushed Into Poverty | CNBC Marathon
youtube_items.summary:
63% of workers say they wouldn't be able to cover a
$500 emergency expense. Most people don't have $1,000 in the bank. Most people cannot handle a tire blowout, or they're
going to put it on credit. Americans now carry more than $1 trillion in credit
card debt. The official poverty measure in the US today is based
on calculations from the mid 1960s. It's also a system that is pervasive in actually
creating poverty situations. Poverty leads to more anxiety. It leads to more stress. It leads to more health problems. 37.9 million. That's the number of Americans currently living in
poverty, accounting for 11.6% of the total population. That's despite the fact that America ranks first as
the richest nation in the world in terms of GDP. The United States is known for having this great
abundance,

analysis.problem: Many people still lack access to safe blood and weaknesses in government blood systems persist
analysis.root_cause: Inequities in access to healthcare and weaknesses in government systems

proposed_actions.action: Improve blood donation infrastructure and increase voluntary donations
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase voluntary blood donations to 90% and reduce blood-related fatalities by 20%

proposed_actions.action: Strengthen government blood systems and address weaknesses
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce weaknesses in government blood systems by 30% and increase access to safe blood by 25%

evidence: New data from the World Health Organization (WHO) show sustained progress towards safer blood supplies globally but also highlight persistent inequalities in access to safe blood and weaknesses in government blood systems
Voluntary donations exceed 85%, but many people still lack access to safe blood
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domain of PLANET (healthcare and access to resources) and HUMAN (well-being and dignity)
analysis.summary: Many people lack access to safe blood due to inequities in healthcare and weaknesses in government systems, requiring improvements in blood donation infrastructure and government systems. The proposed actions aim to increase voluntary donations and strengthen government blood systems.

raw_items.title: WHO issues comprehensive guidelines on filovirus disease, including Ebola and Marburg disease
raw_items.summary: As the Democratic Republic of the Congo is battling an Ebola disease outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) has released its first comprehensive guidelines for the clinical management of filovirus disease which include all types of Ebola and Marburg viruses. The new guidelines highlight the importance of early supportive care to improve patient survival and health outcomes, outlining 16 evidence-based recommendations.

raw_items.title: Open letter to leaders of G7, G20, BRICS and all nations on finalizing the WHO Pandemic Agreement’s Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing annex
raw_items.summary:
Dear Leaders of the G7, the G20, BRICS and of all nations,

We write to you together, from Geneva and from Brasília, with one shared conviction: that the world must finish what it started, and that you can help it do so.

We begin not with an institution or an annex, but with a memory the whole world shares. Not so long ago, our hospitals overflowed. Families said goodbye to the people they loved through glass, or by telephone, or not at all. Children lost grandparents. Docto

raw_items.title: Safe blood supply improves as voluntary donations exceed 85%, but many people still lack access
raw_items.summary: New data from the World Health Organization (WHO) show sustained progress towards safer blood supplies globally but also highlight persistent inequalities in access to safe blood and weaknesses in governance, financing and regulation of blood systems.

raw_items.title: Africa CDC and WHO launch joint continental Ebola response plan
raw_items.summary: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) and the World Health Organization (WHO) today launched a joint continental preparedness and response plan on the ongoing Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus. The plan aims to raise US$ 518 million to support African countries together with partners to prepare for, rapidly

raw_items.title: Young women now have 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death after HPV jab
raw_items.summary: A new study finds that hundreds of lives have been saved since school-age girls were offered the HPV jab in 2008.

raw_items.title: 'We travel 530 miles so our son can have a haircut'
raw_items.summary: How a very special hairdressing salon in Lowestoft is cutting it when it comes to neurodivergence.

raw_items.title: Why you might not be buying the right pain relief for period cramps
raw_items.summary: Many women are buying less effective pain medication for period cramps, supermarket data suggests.

raw_items.title: Resident doctors cancel strike after new offer from government
raw_items.summary: The walkout had been due to start at 07:00 BST on Monday and last until Friday.
